title: Apply for a duty-free vehicle concession
description: Ask to bring in a vehicle without paying customs duty. Available for some individuals and businesses.
summary: Some people and businesses can bring in a vehicle without paying customs duty. There is one form for individuals and one for businesses.
The Barbados Licensing Authority will let you know once your concession is ready.
who_can_apply: an individual who meets the conditions for a duty-free concession (for example, returning national, government employee, person with a disability)
a business authorised by the Government to receive a duty-free concession

items: your name and ID number (or your business name)
your address, phone number and email
the registration number, engine number and chassis number of the vehicle

items.name: Approval letter from the relevant ministry or authority
Bill of sale for the vehicle

fees: There is no application fee. Duty-free status removes customs duties; other fees may still apply.
how_long: You will receive a phone call or email when your document is ready to collect.

options.heading: In person
content: Take the completed Request for Duty Free Concession letter (individual or business version) to the Barbados Licensing Authority.

contact.address:
The Pine, East–West Boulevard
St. Michael
